lyrics: Phone on the counter, nobody called, that's fine

The man I needed to be ain't the man that I became
But the man that I became is the one that kept us sane

Four walls, a laptop, a hundred open tabs
Nobody sees the math behind the thing they say I have

I closed some people off before they could close me out
Thought I was being strategic — turns out I was just scared of doubt

I built a version of myself that function under fire
Buried in so long I forgot I used to dream a little higher

Not complaining, just accounting — there's a difference, you see
The man I had to build is not the one I planned to be

But he's here. He woke up. He handled it.
That counts for something even when the something don't quite fit

The man I had to be and the man I am
Ain't always been the same and I understand
I paid what it cost and I kept it straight
Some things you lose in the making — that's the weight

There was a version of me that used to stay till three
Not grinding — just sitting with what it felt like to be free

I had to learn to be the roof before I had one
Had to be the answer in the room before the question was done

The laugh that didn't serve a purpose, the call I didn't need
The afternoon I let myself just sit without a creed

Price of the package was everything I used to do for free
The man I had to build bolted the door and kept the key

Is this me, or is this the version that survived?
And then I keep going.
Because the answer don't change what's in front of me.

I know what this is, I been here before
You love the access, not the man at the door

Walked in the function, everybody shifted weight
Smiles came out that I ain't seen in quite a great

The ones who never called now got a lot to say
Funny how the picture gets clear when you pave the way

Not paranoid — I'm calibrated, that's the difference here
I been reading rooms since rooms was all I had to fear
I been doing this since long before the crowd appeared
Y'all showed up when the smoke cleared, not when it begun

I remember January when it was just me
The texts that never landed, the rooms I wasn't placed

See the difference in being chosen versus being used
Is one of them shows up before you got nothing to lose

There was one person.
Showed up February, middle of the hard part.
No ask. No agenda. Just — here.
That's the bar now.
Everything else is theater.

Six in the morning, Old National still dark
Headlights on the pavement, got a session and a spark
Ain't running from it — running with it, that's a different thing

The work don't care about the weather or the day
I pull up and get it done — ain't nothing else to say

Rewrote the verse at midnight, scrapped it from the week
If it ain't the truest thing I got then it's too weak

Turned down the feature, said the concept wasn't right
Rather sit with nothing than put something out that's light

I don't need the approval in the comment or the crowd
I hear it in the playback when the mix comes back loud

Still moving, still building, still here
Every morning I show up and make it clear
This ain't luck, this ain't timing, this is work

Corner of Campbellton and Greenbriar, 2019.
Trunk open. Nobody coming.
That was the real start.

The city taught me movement don't mean progress on its own
You can stay in traffic your whole life and never get back home

Atlanta made me patient when the pace felt wrong
I been moving toward this thing my whole life all along

You saw me when I wasn't much to see
Before the work paid off, before the city knew me

Came home to find you'd left the lamp on by the door
Didn't say a word, you just moved over, made the space
Looked at me like I was worth the wait — I memorized that face

You sat through seventeen rewrites of the same verse
You heard the one that didn't land before you heard the first

Most people want the outcome, want to hold the finished thing
You wanted every draft, every doubt, every hard thing

You never walked but I could feel you learning how to wait
Learning how to love someone who's always running late

Not late to you specifically — late to being here
The kind of late that builds up slow across a year

I carry something I don't have the language for just yet
Something past an apology, something past a debt
The closest I can get is: I see it now
What it cost you just to stay — I see it now

You were there for the part I don't talk about in the music.
The man before the man.
And you never asked me to be different.
You just — held the room.

Light came through the blinds a little different this morning
Hard to say exactly why, but something in the air felt new

Felt like something I selected, not inherited or owed
Like I picked this specific weight and chose to carry the load

Pulled up the stems from the record I'd abandoned in the fall
Listened back without the judgment — it was better than I'd called

Something about saying the hard thing out loud to someone real
Freed up something in the process I had forgot to feel

I'm right where I'm supposed to be
Not where I planned, but where I'm free
The work is real, the base is solid
Something lifted, something honest

I'm not striving anymore, I'm arriving every day
Woke up knowing what I'm doing and exactly what to say

I stopped outsourcing the direction to the winds
Everything that happened in the last four tracks was true
The weight, the witness, the watching, the working through

I'm not the man I was — I'm something past the cost
I know exactly what I'm building and I know what it's not

Brooklyn in my blood, Atlanta in my bones
Two cities made me everything I own

Nostrand Ave on a Friday, summer rising off the tar
Corner store on Fulton Street, nobody making it far

You learned to read the room or you got taught how people feel
Big Pun and Biggie on the tape, that was the group

Moved at twelve but kept the accent somewhere in my jaw
The South can hear the Brooklyn if they listen past the drawl

Southwest Atlanta took the Brooklyn and made it slower
Taught me that the depth don't rush, the meaning grows older

I found the gospel in the trap, the hustle in the hymn
The way the 808 slides, that's the theology of him

Brooklyn gave me hunger, made me fast and made me sharp
Atlanta gave me patience, gave me depth, gave me heart

What I make don't sound like Brooklyn and it don't sound like the South
It sounds like the specific thing that lived inside my mouth

I'm not Brooklyn visiting Atlanta, not the A reclaiming New York
I'm the thing that only exists when both cities do the work

Said I wouldn't make it, got that in writing
Now you watching everything I built from the sideline sighting

Sat across the desk from a man who looked right through me
Said the figures didn't add up, said the market wouldn't move me

Wrote it in an email, copied three or four folks in
I still got it saved — that's the kind of thing you keep, my friend

Didn't argue, didn't plead my case, just nodded, left the room
Sometimes the best response is letting time become your proof

Every bar I wrote that year was lit by what he said
I don't need a stage to prove a point — I need the work instead

He reached out through a mutual, said he wanted to connect with me
The email was real polished, talked about a partnership
I read it twice, then closed it — some responses you just skip

Stopped asking for clearance from the people holding gates
Stopped explaining my direction to the people holding plates

The man who wrote me off is now a footnote in my year
I don't say his name — I just let the catalog be clear

I got one question.
And I'm genuinely asking.
What did you think was going to happen?

Sat with everything I made and let it speak without me
Seven records lived before I knew what they would amount to be

I laid it all out flat, looked at what I'd made
For the first time in a while I wasn't scared of what I'd paid

I made it through the making
That's the only thing I know
Every piece that I carried
Had somewhere it needed to go

Lost the dreamer in myself who used to move without a plan
Somewhere in the process turned that dreamer to a man

Don't romanticize the cost — it was real and it was steep
But what grew in place of what I gave is mine to keep

The patience that Atlanta put in me, I couldn't buy
The Brooklyn in my jaw that tells me never wonder why

I don't regret a single pound of what I had to hold
That's the thing about the making — it produces its own gold
Tomorrow morning I'll be back in the booth before the city wakes

Everything I built in the dark is lit enough to see by now
Time to build the next thing — don't need anyone to show me how

Old wood on the pews, the kind that holds the weight of years
Somebody's grandmother two rows up, moving through her prayers

I haven't sat in a place like this in longer than I'd say
Wasn't planning to be here — something just pulled me this way

Just sat down in the back and let whatever's in here be heard
Not revelation, not a sign, not anything I'd claim
Just the specific feeling of a room that knew my name

Something in here knows me
I didn't come with anything
Just showed up and let it hold me
Maybe that's enough
Maybe that's the whole thing

You don't earn your way into the thing that's always there
You just walk in when you're ready and it doesn't even care

Walked out into the Atlanta afternoon a little different
Not saved, not changed, not certain — just shifted

Whatever was in that room came out in my jaw somewhere
I could feel it in the weight of how I moved through the air
Some things don't need explaining, they just need to not be forgot

Fine.
That's the word I keep coming back to.
Not more than that — just fine.

Opened the app before I opened my eyes — old reflex, understand
The number sitting there like it always sits
Not a lot, not a little — just enough that it fits

Expected something — relief maybe, or a rush of something warm
Instead I just stood there in the kitchen in the calm

I remember when the low balance alert would wake me up at three
Heart already moving before my brain could even see

That fear made me precise — it cut away the excess clean
Made every hour count because no hour could be lean

I'd write the verse then check the account then write some more
The fear was in the room with me, behind every open door

Now the account is fine and I wake up and it's just there
I keep waiting for the urgency to walk back in the room

What do you run on
when the thing that drove you
isn't there anymore?

I know how to want and work and push, but not what settled brings
Maybe that's the work now — learning how to move without the weight
Called me on a Wednesday, said the situation changed again

Asked him what he needed, he said time, I said how much, he laughed
That laugh that means he doesn't know
Stood there in the kitchen knowing everything and saying nothing

I can see the whole road from up here
Every turn that's coming, every year
But I can't walk it for him, can't hand it over
All I got is showing up when he needs a soldier

You can't tell someone that, can't speed the clock
All you can do is answer when they knock

I know what gets him out because I know what got me out
But that information isn't mine to hand about

You don't need my experience.
You need your own.
I just want you to know I'm not going anywhere
while you find it.

You teach it by the showing up, by being in the room
Not solving, not advising — just refusing to move
Pick up when he calls me — that's the whole thing, that's the design

They say my name like a warning in the room
Every bar I drop lands like the 1st of the month boom

Can't copy what I do, can't study what I am
Y'all been waiting on the wave — I am the program
Been sharp since before your favorite rapper's career

Pen stay ready, never wasted, every bar precise
Wrote this in the morning, had it perfect by the twice

They said I peaked already — said it with a straight face
I said watch how fast a man can disappear from second place
I move different in the room and in the booth and on the block
I been certified independent since before they started flexin

The work ethic surgical, the focus like a scope
Every verse I write the doubters gotta recalculate their hope

I ain't doing this for the look.
I'm doing this because I'm good at it.
And I've been good at it since before anyone agreed.

I woke up feeling like the work paid off
Like every morning before this was worth the cost

Move different now, but the roots the same
Atlanta sun on Brooklyn bones — that's the name of the game

Friday evening, don't got nowhere to be
First time in a long time that that sentence came free

That's the feeling — old soul wrapped in something new
When the Motown and the trap hit and they both ring true

I grew up on the five-part harmony and the four-four beat
Now I mix it with the 808 and I feel complete

Now I sit with it a minute, let the melody arrive
Let the organ tell me something I wasn't trying to contrive

Marvin had the message and the feel that made it move
Gucci had the swagger and the 808 in the move
I'm somewhere in the middle with a baritone and a prayer

Heard that old record through the speakers on the block
Strings hit first, then the bass, then the 808 knocked

That's the sound that made me — both of those things true
The soul your mother played and the trap that carried through

Grown men crying over records they been knowing since a child
That's the power of a song that was built to run the mile

That specific kind of heavy that don't come from rage
Comes from loss and from the love and from the distance in the run

Something that a grandfather and a grandson understand
Two generations in the room with the same thing hitting right

Moms had the Four Tops on a weeknight in the kitchen
The way the harmony would hit you wasn't something you was wishing

The trap gives it weight, the soul gives it a name
Without the Motown DNA it wouldn't hit the same

Every sliding 808 I write got strings beneath it somewhere
Every string I hear reminds me what the work is actually for

The string hit first.
Then the bass.
Then everything my father and my city ever gave me
landed in the same four bars.

Strings on the trap, 808 on the floor
You ain't built for this — I been built for more

Every syllable I drop is calculated like a function
They thought the Motown loop would soften what I bring
Nah — that string section hits harder than a ring

Bankhead mentality wrapped in a classic loop
That's the combination that'll make the whole crowd droop

Don't confuse the warmth inside the sample for a weakness
I been hard since before the industry found a reason

Two cities on my back and a body of work behind me
The ones who counted me out — they the ones who gonna find me

Put the orchestra beneath the 808 and watch it land
Every head that said it wouldn't work — they don't understand

Walk in any room and the production introduce me first
String section hits the speakers, everybody know the verse

Same strings. Different era.
Same 808. Different man.

This right here is enough.
Just this — the air, the hour, the hum.
Don't need it to be more than what it is.

Porch light on, nobody out there on the block
City doing what it does at ten o'clock

Got a glass of something simple, nothing on the schedule
No call to take, no place to be, no overdue at all

First time in longer than I'd name that I sat outside
Without a reason or an ask or something I was trying to hide

Watched a car slow down and turn and disappear from view
Didn't mean a thing and that was something I could sit with too

I been working on the sitting — that's a work of its own
Learning how to let a good thing be a good thing on its own

The city gave me everything — the drive and the pace
But the city never taught me how to simply hold a space

Two living things just occupying the same street
Not a lesson, not a metaphor, not something I can teach
Just a man on a porch in the city, finally within reach

I spent years building toward something.
And then one evening I was just — here.
And here turned out to be the thing.

She moves through the kitchen like she's known it all her life
Coffee going, something playing low, the morning hitting right

I'm watching from the doorway like I do some days
Taking in the specific way she occupies her space
Seven years of mornings and I'm still learning your face

Woke up knowing I got the right thing
No announcement needed — I got the right thing
All those years of forward and I landed here
Next to someone who was worth the years

There were years she held the weight of what I hadn't built yet
Years she kept the faith when I was too deep in to get it

Never asked me to be something other than the thing I was
Just showed up the way the light shows up — because

Every morning that she held on when leaving would've been the move
I'm still here because she gave me somewhere I could prove

You didn't ask me to be ready.
You just held on until I was.
I see that.
Last March I had a number in my phone I couldn't dial
Something 'bout watching people eat and laugh while you ain't there yet
Told myself a hundred days, I'll be the one they can't forget
But August felt like something locked inside my chest just broke and opened
I stopped explaining myself somewhere 'round quarter three

Not bitter, not forgiving — just done calculating what I'm owed
I just loaded up and took a breath and let the whole thing go

Second wind — I found a second wind
When they stopped watching that's exactly when I started again
Had to lose the room to figure out I never needed them
Everything I built with no applause still got me to the end

Same carpet, same fluorescent bulb, same picture hanging crooked on the side
Difference is this time my name was on the placard at the table
That Tuesday changed my whole approach — I stopped playing safe that week

The answer is I stopped dividing what I had between the doubt
Poured it all one direction and just waited for the drought to clear out
Left the house at ten, had a bag to move by midnight sharp

Everybody in that building know exactly why I came
Walked straight past the velvet rope and never had to say my name

Six months I was eating off a hotplate in the A
Now the section on the left is what I paid for yesterday

I move with a specific kind of weight that clears a path
Not aggressive, not announcing — I just do the math

On my way, on my way
Can't stop me now, not today

That's how it goes when paths split clean and nobody held a grudge
You just keep your eyes ahead and let the city be the judge

This the part they don't discuss — the discipline inside the glow
All those early Tuesday nights that nobody got to know
Every no I got back then just added fuel to where I went

Started off the night with forty dollars and a half a tank
Ended up at Compound posted by the upper rank
That's the kind of night that makes you want to write the date down somewhere

That's Atlanta doing what she does when everything aligns
You never plan it, never book it — just show up and read the signs

Run it back, run it back
Everything we did tonight, run it back

That specific type of joy you share with maybe one or two
Not the kind you post about, it lives inside the chest

Proof that it gets good again when it don't feel like it will
That's the shot that keeps me level — that's the actual real deal

Third time they pulled the rug I didn't even check the floor
Just looked ahead and mapped the quickest path back to the door

Had a mentor tell me once that I was built for smaller things
Shook his hand, walked to the car, drove home and cut the strings

I don't carry what I can't use past a certain point
When the weight stops teaching you it's time to dislocate the joint

Gods don't fall, gods don't fall
You can shake the whole building, we don't fall
You can sit across the table quiet 'cause you already freed them

Fixed the car by Thursday, rebuilt the deal by Friday night
The person — let 'em go — some exits save your life

It feels like nothing
Not numb — just... past it
That's not invincible — that's just trained

Walked in wearing nothing special, whole room shifted anyway
That's not swagger, that's a posture that took years to get that way

Got a call at eight this morning from a number trying threats
Blocked it, ate my breakfast, made a hundred grand by ten

The thing about a target is it only works if you stand still
I've been moving since they drew it — never gave 'em time to get a feel
Untouchable — everything they threw just went right through

Pulled up to the spot where somebody said I wasn't wanted
Stayed two hours, left with three connects, nobody confronted

Had a partner flip the script on a deal we built together
Took my half, rebuilt the other side, did it better

Watched a man spend eighteen months constructing my collapse
While I was two time zones over building something he can't map

I don't even think about it anymore
That's what untouchable actually is — not that nothing reaches you
just that nothing holds

We're in different orbits now
Same city, different clouds

Last time I saw you it was March outside the BP on Flat Shoals
You had a new car, I had a new number, neither one of us called
It's just the version of me that needed that specific person isn't here

I don't know if that's loss or if it's just the shape of time
How two people move in parallel and then diverge the line

My therapist said that's healthy — I said yeah maybe so
But healthy doesn't always mean it doesn't sting a little when you go

Deleted it last August on a Sunday with the windows cracked
Felt lighter by the Tuesday and I haven't looked back
Some connections are a chapter and the chapter's just done

That felt like closure in the produce section under fluorescent lights
Which is probably the most Atlanta ending to a story I could write

Six AM before the house wakes up I'm already at the desk
Running numbers in my head before I've even had a breath

My mother calls on Sundays and I answer every time
Even when I'm stretched so thin that I can barely hold the line
The one who answers when it rings, begins again, begins

There's a particular kind of tired that don't show up on your face
It lives somewhere behind the eyes in a very specific place

I carry the weight, I carry the weight
Not because I have to — just the way it was made

Had a Wednesday where four things landed on my plate at once
A funeral, a deal, a family matter, and a front
I handled all of it by seven, ate alone at eight

Nobody knew that Wednesday happened — I didn't tell a soul
That's not stoic, that's just how you move when you've been playing this role

But lately I've been asking what I'm actually building toward
Is it something I believe in or just motion I can't afford to stop?

He just looked at me and said you just do
And then one day you realize the weight's the only thing
that proved you were actually here
